section_name: Period of Suspension or Revocation; Reinstatement of License
text:
(a) Revocation of a license is for an indefinite period.

(b) Except as provided by Subsection (c) or Subchapter O, the department may not suspend a license for a period that exceeds one year.

(c) The department may not reinstate a license revoked under Section 521.294 (Department’s Determination for License Revocation) (5) until the court that filed the report for which the license was revoked files an additional report on final disposition of the case.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 521. Driver's Licenses & Certificates
##### § 521.312 — Period of Suspension or Revocation; Reinstatement of License

(a) Revocation of a license is for an indefinite period.

(b) Except as provided by Subsection (c) or Subchapter O, the department may not suspend a license for a period that exceeds one year.

(c) The department may not reinstate a license revoked under Section 521.294 (Department’s Determination for License Revocation) (5) until the court that filed the report for which the license was revoked files an additional report on final disposition of the case.

*Acts 2019, 86th Leg., R.S., Ch. 710 (H.B.162), Sec. 2, eff. September 1, 2019.*

subsections.text: The department may not reinstate a license revoked under Section 521.294 (Department’s Determination for License Revocation) (5) until the court that filed the report for which the license was revoked files an additional report on final disposition of the case.
